pattern.name: document_to_object_extraction
pattern.description: Use this pattern when a business document describes one or more entities that should become business object JSON artifacts.

steps.step: List attributes and constraints for each entity
Map the resulting model to repository-supported JSON shape

pattern.name: catalog_generation
pattern.description: Use this pattern when generated business object files need to be referenced by a stable catalog for later packaging.

steps.step: Collect generated file paths from business-objects/generated/[context]/ for the current business context
Update or create business-objects/catalog/master.catalog.json with stable references to all generated contexts

pattern.name: ambiguity_tracking
pattern.description: Use this pattern when the source blueprint does not fully define field types, required status, or relationships.

steps.step: Apply the most conservative repository-aligned interpretation
Record the ambiguity in a summary or report artifact when needed

pattern.name: context_based_grouping
pattern.description: Use this pattern when the user provides a domain, module, or bounded context that should drive output folder organization.

steps.step: Use the user-provided context as the primary grouping key
Place related business object files under business-objects/generated/[context]/
Create a matching context-level catalog entry at business-objects/catalog/[context].catalog.json

pattern.name: entity
pattern.source_signal: Named business noun with described attributes

pattern.name: embedded_structure
pattern.source_signal: Repeated sub-structure always used inside a parent object
pattern.target_shape: Nested structure or dedicated reusable object based on repository conventions

pattern.name: enumeration
pattern.source_signal: Closed set of allowed values such as status or category
pattern.target_shape: Enum-like definition or constrained field representation based on existing repository support

pattern.name: reference
pattern.source_signal: Business entity linked to another independently meaningful entity

pattern.name: source_and_generated_separation
pattern.rule: Keep source blueprints under business-blueprints/ separate from generated business object artifacts under business-objects/
pattern.benefit: Prevents accidental confusion between human-authored source and generated output

pattern.name: catalog_as_handoff_contract
pattern.rule: Use business-objects/catalog/[context].catalog.json and business-objects/catalog/master.catalog.json as the dedicated catalog contract for later packaging workflows
pattern.benefit: Creates a clean contract between generation mode and packaging mode

anti_pattern.name: Directly packaging after generation
anti_pattern.why_bad: It collapses generation and packaging responsibilities into one workflow and makes the handoff boundary unclear.
anti_pattern.correct_approach: Stop after generation and hand off packaging work to baw-package-manager.

anti_pattern.name: Generating business objects without checking repository examples
anti_pattern.why_bad: It can create incompatible JSON shapes that existing import logic cannot use.
anti_pattern.correct_approach: Inspect existing examples and helper functions before generating artifacts.

anti_pattern.name: Hiding assumptions inside generated files
anti_pattern.why_bad: Users lose traceability when field meanings or relationships were inferred.
anti_pattern.correct_approach: Expose assumptions in summaries or companion report artifacts.
